original_hypothesis.title: Any Single Owner Can Unilaterally Capture Wallet by Adding Self and Removing All Others
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The MultiOwnable access control uses a flat model where any single owner has full administrative authority: addOwnerAddress, addOwnerPublicKey, removeOwnerAtIndex, execute, executeBatch, and upgradeToAndCall. There is no threshold, multisig requirement, or time delay. A single compromised or rogue owner can: (1) add their own additional addresses as owners, (2) remove all other legitimate owners via removeOwnerAtIndex, and (3) upgrade the implementation to arbitrary code. This one-of-N trust model means compromising ANY owner compromises the ENTIRE wallet.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Combined with cross-chain replay of owner changes (addOwner/removeOwner are whitelisted in canSkipChainIdValidation), a single owner compromise on any chain propagates to all chains simultaneously.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _checkOwner only verifies isOwnerAddress(msg.sender) || msg.sender == address(this) - any single owner passes; no threshold mechanism

preconditions: One owner is compromised or turns malicious
No external governance or monitoring prevents rapid owner changes

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Flat 1-of-N access model with no threshold or timelock on owner management
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Any single owner can perform full takeover

operational_questions: Are there off-chain monitoring systems for owner change events?
Is there a time delay between owner changes that could allow intervention?
How many wallets have multiple owners where this 1-of-N model is the weakest link?
validation_requirements: Execute owner addition, removal of all others, and fund drain in single batch
Test cross-chain propagation of owner changes via replayable UserOps
Verify no on-chain safeguard prevents rapid sequential owner changes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise any single wallet owner key
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call addOwnerAddress with attacker backup addresses
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Call removeOwnerAtIndex for all legitimate owners
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Execute arbitrary transactions to drain wallet; replay owner changes cross-chain

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete wallet takeover across all chains
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All legitimate owners locked out permanently
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No on-chain recovery; requires social/legal intervention
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All wallet operations on all chains

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: None - single owner can execute everything

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Rapid AddOwner/RemoveOwner events in same block
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: New owner addresses immediately followed by fund movements

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
